# Dynatrace SE AI Workspace

A pre-configured AI assistant workspace for Dynatrace observability workflows, compatible with both Claude Code and GitHub Copilot. Skills, prompts, and MCP server connections are ready to use — load the workspace and start investigating without any additional setup.

## Core Purpose

This workspace teaches AI assistants how to work with a live Dynatrace environment. It combines domain knowledge (agent skills), reusable task templates (prompts), direct platform access via the Dynatrace MCP server, and the dtctl CLI for terminal-level operations — so an AI can answer observability questions, run DQL queries, investigate problems, and verify results end-to-end without additional configuration.

## Key Components

**MCP Servers:**
Two live Dynatrace environments are pre-wired: `production-mcp` (default) and `sprint-mcp` (fallback). Specify which server to use at the start of a session.

**dtctl CLI:**
A kubectl-style CLI for Dynatrace that complements MCP-based investigations with terminal-level access. Used for verification steps — confirming notebooks and other artifacts created by AI workflows actually exist and are correctly structured in Dynatrace.

**Agent Skills:**
16 domain knowledge packages load automatically when relevant. They cover: DQL syntax rules and query patterns (dt-dql-essentials); RED metrics and runtime telemetry for Java/.NET/Node.js/Python/PHP/Go (dt-obs-services); RUM, Web Vitals, user sessions, mobile crashes, and frontend errors (dt-obs-frontends); distributed traces, spans, service dependencies, and request flow (dt-obs-tracing); host CPU/memory/disk/network/containers and process-level telemetry (dt-obs-hosts); Kubernetes pods, workloads, OOMKills, scheduling, and security posture (dt-obs-kubernetes); AWS EC2/RDS/Lambda/ECS/VPC/S3/DynamoDB/SQS and cost optimization (dt-obs-aws); Azure VMs/AKS/Functions/VNet/Event Hubs/Container Apps/Key Vault (dt-obs-azure); GCP Compute/GKE/Cloud Run/Pub/Sub/IAM/Secret Manager (dt-obs-gcp); DAVIS problem analysis, root cause identification, and impact assessment (dt-obs-problems); time series forecasting, capacity saturation planning, and trend/anomaly detection (dt-obs-predictive-analytics); dashboard and notebook JSON creation, modification, and analysis (dt-app-dashboards, dt-app-notebooks); classic entity DQL to Smartscape migration (dt-migration); and Dynatrace CLI resource lifecycle and automation (dtctl).

**Prompts / Slash Commands:**
7 task templates cover common workflows: daily standup, health check, error investigation, problem troubleshooting, incident response, performance regression, and standup with notebook creation (the last one combines MCP-based analysis, Dynatrace notebook creation, and dtctl verification in a single end-to-end workflow).

## Operational Rule

Always start with Dynatrace problems before querying logs or metrics. The built-in prompts enforce this automatically.

## Technical Details

Skills follow a `dt-<domain>[-<usecase>]` naming convention with a `SKILL.md` and optional `references/` subdirectory. Load `dt-dql-essentials` before writing any DQL queries. The `dtctl` skill covers the Dynatrace CLI for resource lifecycle (apply, delete, share, history, restore), workflow and analyzer execution, and bulk or CI/CD-style automation — use it when operations aren't exposed via MCP or when scripting is the goal.
